key_insight: An MLIP-trained, CALPHAD-based workflow can rapidly map spinodal instability and predict microstructure evolution across high-dimensional alloy composition space using analytic Gibbs-energy derivatives.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more scalable design of spinodal alloys by coupling thermodynamic stability screening with phase-field microstructure prediction.
method: Train MLIP models to supply thermodynamic and elastic constants for candidate alloy compositions.
Compute analytic Gibbs-energy Hessians to accelerate and improve spinodal stability analysis versus finite differences.
Feed the MLIP-derived thermodynamics into an elasto-chemical phase-field simulation to predict morphology.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a demonstrated application to microstructure stability in the Hf-Nb-Ti-V quaternary system.
watch_item: The workflow depends on the fidelity and transferability of the MLIP/CALPHAD inputs, so accuracy may degrade outside the composition and phase space covered by training and parameterization.
paper_title: From MLIPs to Microstructure: A High-Throughput Computational Framework to Design Spinodal Alloys in High-Dimensional Composition Spaces via Analytic Derivatives of CALPHAD Model Predictions
